pi_items.source: task.json instructions and verifier.py EXPECTED_SPLIT_Z logic
setup_task.sh copies it to this path
verifier.py calculates volume but contains absolutely no logic to enforce volume constraints

privileged_info_summary: The data is not synthetic; the T8_housing_bracket.FCStd is an authentic CAD model created by FreeCAD contributor @hasecilu in 2022. The verifier correctly enforces the output STL paths and the 15mm split plane requirement. However, the task description and verifier comments are flawed due to the author incorrectly assuming the bracket is roughly 60mm tall (Z=[0, 60]). In reality, the BRep point data reveals the bracket is only 30mm tall, spanning Z=[-14.0, 16.0]. Because the verifier script only checks the boundary edges matching the split plane (z_max for the bottom part and z_min for the top part) and not their full extent, splitting the model at Z=15mm validly produces a 29mm-thick bottom part (Z=[-14, 15]) and a bizarre 1mm-thick top slice (Z=[15, 16]) that still successfully passes all verifier checks. The 10% volume tolerance claim in the metadata is entirely ignored by the verifier.
